title: EthernetIpAdapter component configuration
description: The configuration EthernetIpAdapter itself understands: the object at `component.global`, with each entry of `component.instances[]` validated against `#/$defs/device`. A southbound EtherNet/IP adapter models ONE DEVICE PER INSTANCE (one PLC / CIP endpoint) â€” how to reach it, which CIP tags or assembly fields map to which signals, and what it is permitted to write. Each device runs in one of two modes: `poll` (the default â€” scheduled explicit-messaging reads of CIP tags, grouped by cadence) or `push` (class-1 implicit I/O â€” the device produces an assembly at the RPI and its byte-offset fields become signals). A poll device declares `pollGroups[]` and no `io`; a push device declares `io` and no `pollGroups[]`. See DESIGN.md and docs/SOUTHBOUND.md for the contract this implements. The library envelope (logging, messaging, metricEmission, heartbeat, streaming, credentials, parameters, health, hierarchy, identity, topic, tags, and the `component.token` UNS override) is owned by the canonical schema and is deliberately not redeclared here.

defaults.description: Defaults applied to every device/group that does not override them (precedence: signal â–¸ group â–¸ device.defaults â–¸ global.defaults â–¸ built-in).

pollIntervalMs.description: Poll cadence for any group that does not override it.

publishMode.description: Meaning: `onChange` publishes only samples that pass the deadband/change gate; `always` publishes every polled sample.

batchMs.description: Coalescing window: samples for one signal within the window ride one SouthboundSignalUpdate.samples[]. 0 = publish per poll cycle.

reconnectBackoffMinMs.description: The first reconnect window. Each attempt doubles it, up to the max.

reconnectBackoffMaxMs.description: The reconnect ceiling. Backoff is jittered within the window, so a plant full of adapters does not reconnect in lockstep when a PLC reboots.

staleSignalSecs.description: A signal with no successful (GOOD) read for longer than this counts as stale. Suspended while an instance is paused.

keepaliveProbeIntervalMs.description: Paused-state liveness probe cadence: while paused, the adapter keeps `connected` truthful with a slow real CIP round-trip on this interval.

metricsIntervalSecs.description: Operational-metrics emit cadence, seconds. Interval counters reset on each emit; totals never reset.

security.title: Connection security (CIP Security)
security.description: TLS on the explicit-messaging path (CIP Security). Absent â‡’ plaintext. `mode: tls` wraps the EtherNet/IP session in mutual TLS on TCP 2221; it is rejected on a `mode: push` (class-1 I/O) instance. Cert/key/CA material comes from the credentials vault (`certSecret`/`ca.secret`) or from files (`certFile`/`keyFile`/`ca.file`).

mode.description: Meaning: `plaintext` (default) â‡’ EtherNet/IP on TCP 44818. `tls` â‡’ EtherNet/IP over TLS (mutual X.509) on TCP 2221 (the default when the endpoint has no explicit port).

client.description: The adapter's own identity for mutual TLS (required for `mode: tls`). Use `certSecret` (a vault TLS bundle) OR `certFile` + `keyFile`.

certSecret.description: A credentials-vault secret name holding a `{certPem, keyPem[, caPem]}` TLS bundle.

keyFile.description: A PEM client private-key file path.

ca.description: Trust anchors for verifying the device certificate. Use `secret` (a vault PEM secret) OR `file`; may be omitted when `certSecret` carries `caPem`, or when `verifyPeer` is false.

secret.description: A credentials-vault secret name holding CA certificate PEM (one or more roots).

verifyPeer.description: Meaning: `true` (default) â‡’ verify the device certificate against the trust anchors. `false` â‡’ accept any device certificate (a loud, event-raising commissioning/debug posture).

serverName.description: The verification / SNI name; default = the endpoint host. An IP literal is verified against the device certificate's IP SAN.

checkExpiration.description: Meaning: `true` (default) â‡’ reject an expired / not-yet-valid device certificate. `false` â‡’ tolerate it (for RTC-less devices).

cipherSuites.description: Optional cipher-suite allow-list (IANA / rustls names, e.g. `TLS_ECDHE_ECDSA_WITH_AES_128_GCM_SHA256`). Default = the spec-current GCM + TLS 1.3 suites. CBC/NULL/PSK suites are not supported.

device.title: One device
device.description: One entry of `component.instances[]`. One device == one PLC / CIP endpoint, with its own task and connection lifecycle.

allOf.description: Mode is exclusive: a `push` device requires `io` and must not declare `pollGroups`; any other device (poll â€” the default when `mode` is absent) requires `pollGroups` and must not declare `io`.

id.description: Unique device id. It is the `{instance}` token of this device's UNS topics and the `instance` dimension of its metrics, so it must be a valid UNS token (lower-kebab) and stable.

adapter.description: Which protocol backend to use. `ethernet-ip` is the CIP explicit-messaging backend; `sim` selects the in-process simulator. Matches `DeviceBackend::kind()` and is published in the `device.adapter` field of every SouthboundSignalUpdate.

mode.description: Meaning: `poll` (default) reads CIP tags on a schedule â€” requires `pollGroups[]` and forbids `io`. `push` consumes a class-1 implicit-I/O assembly the device produces at the RPI â€” requires `io` and forbids `pollGroups[]`. One instance = one device = one mode.

connection.description: How to reach the device. Deliberately OPEN â€” every protocol needs different keys, and this is the one place the adapter is not strict. Everything else stays `additionalProperties: false`.

endpoint.description: `<host>` or `<host>:<port>` (default CIP port 44818). Published in the `device.endpoint` field.

slot.description: ControlLogix CPU slot â‡’ backplane connection path (`1,<slot>`). Absent â‡’ no routing path (correct for cpppo / CompactLogix-direct).

connected.description: Meaning: `true` â‡’ CIP connected messaging (ForwardOpen); `false` (default) â‡’ unconnected explicit messaging.

pollGroups.description: Poll mode only: the device's poll groups; each is read on its own cadence. Required for `mode: poll`, forbidden for `mode: push`.

io.description: Push mode only: the class-1 implicit-I/O connection + assembly layout. Required for `mode: push`, forbidden for `mode: poll`.

allow.description: Stable `signal.id`s this device may write â€” a CIP tag path (poll) or an `a<assemblyInstance>/<offset>/<type>` output-field id (push). Anything not listed is refused, whatever a command asks for. An empty list â€” the default â€” means the device is read-only, the correct posture for anything touching a control system.

pollGroup.title: A poll group
pollGroup.description: A set of signals read together on one cadence.

id.description: The `pollGroup` metric dimension. Defaults to `group-<n>` (1-based) when absent.

pollIntervalMs.description: This group's cadence; falls to device â–¸ global â–¸ built-in (5000).

publishMode.description: This group's publish gate; falls to device â–¸ global â–¸ built-in (onChange).

signal.title: A signal
signal.description: One CIP tag mapped to an EdgeCommons data point.

name.description: Human label AND the `data` topic channel. Lower-kebab (a valid UNS token), unique per device.

tagPath.description: The CIP tag path, verbatim/case-sensitive (`LINE_SPEED`, `Program:Main.FillPV`). It IS the stable `signal.id`. Unique per device.

type.description: Meaning: the CIP elementary type used to decode the tag. `string`, structures/UDTs, and multi-dimensional arrays are not supported and are rejected.

arrayCount.description: Present â‡’ the signal is a 1-D array read of that many elements; the value is a JSON array.

scale.description: Published value = `raw * scale + offset` (element-wise for arrays; numeric types only, not `bool`).

deadband.description: The change/deadband gate for `onChange` publishing (numeric types only).

type.description: Meaning: `none` republishes on any change; `absolute` requires `|newâˆ’old| â‰¥ value`; `percent` is relative to the previous value. Arrays: exceeded if ANY element exceeds.

io.title: Push-mode I/O block
io.description: The class-1 implicit-I/O connection + assembly layout for a `mode: push` device. The device produces its input (Tâ†’O) assembly at the RPI; the adapter consumes it and maps the configured byte-offset fields to signals. The optional output (Oâ†’T) assembly carries values the adapter produces toward the device. Field bounds are validated against the assembly size at startup.

rpiMs.description: Requested Tâ†’O RPI (the device's produce cadence toward the adapter), ms. The negotiated API from the ForwardOpen reply is what actually runs.

o2tRpiMs.description: Requested Oâ†’T RPI (the adapter's produce cadence toward the device), ms. Defaults to `rpiMs`.

connectionType.description: Meaning: Tâ†’O connection type. `multicast` consume joins the group from the ForwardOpen reply's sockaddr item; Oâ†’T is always point-to-point.

timeoutMultiplier.description: Inactivity watchdog = multiplier Ã— Tâ†’O API. One of the eight CIP-legal multipliers (mapped to the wire code `4 << n`).

assemblies.description: The assembly instance ids. `output`/`input` are the Oâ†’T / Tâ†’O connection points; `config` is included in the connection path when present (most targets require it).

config.description: Config assembly instance (connection path only; no data plane).

output.description: Oâ†’T assembly instance (the adapter's outputs; the Oâ†’T connection point). Also the `a<inst>/â€¦` prefix of output-field ids.

input.description: Tâ†’O assembly instance (the device's inputs to the adapter; the Tâ†’O connection point). Also the `a<inst>/â€¦` prefix of input-field ids.
The Tâ†’O (input) direction â€” the device's data to the adapter.

sizeBytes.description: Negotiated Tâ†’O data size in bytes (excl. sequence/header â€” the crate adds those per format). A received frame that mismatches is dropped and counted, never partially decoded.

realTimeFormat.description: Meaning: Tâ†’O framing. Conventional targets produce `modeless`; `header32` carries a run/idle header.

sampleMs.description: Publish-eligibility floor per field, ms: at most one sample per field per window (0 = every accepted frame is eligible). The anti-flood gate for fast RPIs; deadband/publishMode apply after it.

output.description: The Oâ†’T (output) direction â€” the adapter's data to the device. Absent â‡’ a heartbeat Oâ†’T connection (no output data).

sizeBytes.description: Oâ†’T data size in bytes. `0` â‡’ a heartbeat connection (no output data; `signals` must be absent).

realTimeFormat.description: Meaning: Oâ†’T framing; `header32` carries the run/idle bit.

run.description: Initial run/idle state produced in the 32-bit header.

signals.description: Output-assembly fields (writable via `sb/write` when allow-listed). Same field shape as input signals, minus `deadband`.

ioField.title: An assembly-layout field
ioField.description: One byte-offset field within a push assembly (the push analog of a poll signal). The stable `signal.id` is `a<assemblyInstance>/<offset>/<type>[.<bit>]`; `name` is the `data` topic channel and friendly label.

name.description: Human label AND the `data` topic channel. Lower-kebab (a valid UNS token), unique per device.

offset.description: Byte offset of the field within the assembly data. Fields may overlap (a status word plus its bits); every field must fit inside `sizeBytes`.

type.description: Meaning: the CIP elementary type used to decode/encode the field. `string`, structures/UDTs, and multi-dimensional arrays are not supported and are rejected.

bit.description: Bit extraction (0â€“7) within the byte at `offset`. `bool` only, single element (no `arrayCount`).

arrayCount.description: Present â‡’ a contiguous array of that many elements; the value is a JSON array.

scale.description: Published value = `raw * scale + valueOffset` (element-wise for arrays; numeric types only, not `bool`).

valueOffset.description: The additive term of the value transform (see `scale`). Named `valueOffset` to avoid colliding with the byte `offset`.

deadband.description: The change/deadband gate for `onChange` publishing (input-side fields only; numeric types).

type.description: Meaning: `none` republishes on any change; `absolute` requires `|newâˆ’old| â‰¥ value`; `percent` is relative to the previous value. Arrays: exceeded if ANY element exceeds.
